c74object.name: stoplight
c74object.digest: A data gate and bundler for Max messages
c74object.description: The `stoplight` object is a data gate that either passes through incoming messages or queues them based on a control signal. It supports multiple data inlet/outlet pairs. When the control inlet (the rightmost inlet) is 0, messages from Inlet 1 (the leftmost inlet) are passed immediately to Outlet 1, bundled with the most recent messages received on the other data inlets (2 to N). When the control signal is non-zero, incoming messages from Inlet 1 and the current values of the other data inlets are stored together in a FIFO (First-In-First-Out) queue. As soon as the control signal returns to 0, all queued bundles are released in the order they were received, with each bundle's contents sent out their respective outlets in right-to-left order.

inlet.digest: Inlet 1 (Hot): Data to be passed or queued.
inlet.description: Accepts any Max message. These messages are either passed immediately to the outlet (along with values from other data inlets) or stored in a FIFO queue depending on the state of the control inlet.

inlet.digest: Inlet 2..N (Cold): Data to be bundled with Inlet 1.
inlet.description: The most recent value received on each of these inlets is bundled with any message entering Inlet 1. These inlets do not trigger output themselves.
A control value. When 0, data entering Inlet 1 is passed immediately. When non-zero, incoming data is queued. If a non-zero value changes back to 0, the queue is immediately flushed.

outlet.description: The data outputs from the gate. Each outlet corresponds to a data inlet pair. When a bundle is released, messages are sent out their respective outlets in right-to-left order.

objarg.name: extra-pairs
objarg.digest: The number of extra data inlet/outlet pairs to add to the default pair (1-9).
